content.problemStatement:
Chef is a software developer, so he has to switch between different languages sometimes. Each programming language has some features, which are represented by integers here.

Currently, Chef has to use a language with two given features AAA and BBB. He has two options --- switching to a language with two features A1A_1A1​ and B1B_1B1​, or to a language with two features A2A_2A2​ and B2B_2B2​. All four features of these two languages are pairwise distinct.

Tell Chef whether he can use the first language, the second language or neither of these languages (if no single language has all the required features).


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains six space-separated integers A,B,A1,B1,A2,B2A, B, A_1, B_1, A_2, B_2A,B,A1​,B1​,A2​,B2​.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the integer 111 if Chef should switch to the first language, or 222 if Chef should switch to the second language, or 000 if Chef cannot switch to either language.
constraints: A1,B1,A2,B2A_1, B_1, A_2, B_2A1​,B1​,A2​,B2​ are pairwise distinct

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The first language has the required features --- features 111 and 222.
Example case 2: The second language has the required features --- features 333 and 444.
Example case 3: Neither language has both features 111 and 222.
